question_text: Etymology: a Greek word meaning room made its way to Latin and other Romance languages. From there it reached farther shores in the colonial era. You know this word with a very different meaning, referring to to an everyday device. What device?
answer_text: Camera (and the Hindi word 'kamra' for room, both from the Greek 'kamara')
confirmation_text: Yes. Camera and kamra come from the same Greek word

discussion.text: Yes. Camera and kamra come from the same Greek word

question_text: On similar lines... Which European country's name is believed by some to be derived from the name of an ancient Indian river that's also the name of a goddess? We know the country better by its anglecized name
answer_text: Croatia (Hrvatska), derived from the river Saraswati (Haravathi)
confirmation_text: Croatia it is, or Hrvatska in Croatian

discussion.text: Switzerland after Saraswati, and the non anglicized name is some Helvetica something?
River is correct, country isnt Switzerland
I am assuming it’s other names of Saraswati
Very close on non anglecized name
they call it Hrvatska or something like that
Croatia it is, or Hrvatska in Croatian
Derives from Haravathi, an old name from Saraswati

question_text: She is considered the first notable woman in western mathematics. As a Neoplatonist, she headed up the Platonist school at Alexandria and taught mathematics, philosophy and astronomy. Her brutal murder at the hands of a Christian mob in the 5th century CE is considered by some scholars to mark the end of classical antiquity and the beginning of the Middle Ages. She was the daughter of the last head of the legendary Library of Alexandria. Name this mathematician who was the victim of the first documented witch-hunt.

discussion.text: Aditi posted a nice image of women scientists in the Random Musings channel, that is what prompted the question

question_text: The world isthmus – which has come to mean any short, narrow stretch of land that connects two large land masses – is derived from the name of this thin four mile stretch that connects the Peloponnesos peninsula to the European mainland. Name this original isthmus and the country it is in.

A _ is a large marine inlet or channel that is wider than a fjord, deeper than a bay, and often separates land from an island, or connects to an ocean in multiple places. What water body are we talking about ?

discussion.text: I first heard about this when visiting Martha's Vineyard. Nantucket sound

question_text: When ______ first proposed the idea in public, it failed to garner much support. Over a year later, he made a speech in a football/handegg stadium that was a turning point in public support for the idea. Ted Sorenson wrote the speech for him, and _____ jotted down the words
in between the lines of typed copy. It is quite likely that you have seen an excerpt of this speech. Who, and what concept was he pitching? Both blanks are the name of the same person.
answer_text: John F. Kennedy (JFK); the proposal to go to the Moon (Apollo program)

discussion.text: Os this about why do we choose to go to the moon

question_text: Easy question, hopefully: This Indian politician translated the autobiography of Marie Curie into Hindi while he was imprisoned during the independence movement. He was arrested and jailed for anti-government protests while still a minor. He dropped his original caste-derived surname Srivastava, so we know him by the bachelor's degree he was awarded at Kashi Vidyapeeth. As Home Minister of his state, he ordered policemen to stop using lathis for crowd control and use jets of water instead. The only time he was seen in public not wearing a dhoti, he wore pyjamas to a dinner in honor of Queen Elizabeth II. Who?
